header_info.title: Global Oil and Gas Plant Tracker - January 2026
header_info.citation: Recommended Citation: "Global Energy Monitor, Global Oil and Gas Plant Tracker, January 2026 release." (See the CC license for attribution requirements if sharing or adapting the data set.)
header_info.contact: Contact: Jenny Martos, Project Manager, Global Energy Monitor - jenny.martos@globalenergymonitor.org

entries.name: announced
entries.definition: Projects that have been publicly reported but have not yet moved actively forward by applying for permits or seeking land, material, or financing.

entries.name: pre-construction
entries.definition: Projects that are actively moving forward in seeking governmental approvals, land rights, or financing.

entries.name: construction
entries.definition: Site preparation and other activities are underway.

entries.name: operating
entries.definition: The plant has been formally commissioned; commercial operation has begun.

entries.name: shelved
entries.definition: Two years have passed with no action on a project after the project proposal.

entries.name: cancelled
entries.definition: A cancellation announcement has been made. The status is marked as ‘cancelled – inferred 4 y’ if no progress has been observed for at least four years.

entries.name: mothballed
entries.definition: The project is disused, but not dismantled.

entries.name: retired
entries.definition: The project has been decommissioned; this term is also used if the plant has been destroyed by war.

entries.name: GEM location ID
entries.definition: Internal generated ID for the power station (non-unique)

entries.name: GEM unit ID
entries.definition: Internal generated ID for the unit (unique)

entries.name: Wiki URL
entries.definition: GEM.wiki URL for plant

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: Country/area location of the power station (unabbreviated). The Country/Area column presents energy data within various economic contexts, and the geographical unit does not strictly follow political boundaries.

entries.name: Plant name
entries.definition: Commonly used name

entries.name: Plant Name in Local Language / Script
entries.definition: Commonly used name in the locally used script

entries.name: Other Name(s)
entries.definition: Alternative names

entries.name: Unit name
entries.definition: Sequential numbers, grouped by blocks with emdash is necessary (e.g. 1-1, 2-1)

entries.name: Fuel
entries.definition: All fuel types supported. See Abbreviations tab for mapping to previous abbreviations.

entries.name: Capacity (MW)
entries.definition: Installed capacity of the unit, in megawatts

entries.name: Status
entries.definition: Current status of plant, see definitions in About tab

entries.name: Turbine/Engine Technology
entries.definition: Equipment technology. Options include: internal combustion, combined cycle, gas turbine, internal combustion combined cycle, integrated solar combined cycle, steam turbine, Allam-Fetvedt Cycle

entries.name: Equipment Manufacturer/Model
entries.definition: Equpment manufacturer and model.

entries.name: CHP
entries.definition: Combined heat and power/cogeneration. Options include: yes, no, not found

entries.name: Hydrogen capable?
entries.definition:
Hydrogen capable: turbine - The turbine at the gas plant is capable of burning some % of hydrogen. 
Hydrogen capable: progress - The gas plant has made some sort of progress beyond having a gas turbine that is hydrogen capable. This could include partnerships to secure hydrogen, announcements towards hydrogen infrastructure, or an actual hydrogen contract.
Using hydrogen - The plant is using hydrogen as a fuel.
N - The plant is not capable of using hydrogen. 
not found - no information found

entries.name: CCS attachment?
entries.definition: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is most relevant for in development projects. Options include: yes, no, not found

entries.name: Conversion/replacement?
entries.definition:
Options include: 
conversion - converts the steam turbine of an existing plant to burn gas.
replacement - existing plant is retired and replaced with a new  gas-fired plant. 
change of plans - cases in which one or more  units were planned and those plans were cancelled and subsequently gas units were planned at the same location.
use of ST in CC set - cases where there is a steam turbine (usually coal) and is then repurposed to be part of a gas-fired combined cycle unit. Can also be referred to as “repowering” into a combined cycle where gas turbine(s) and HSRGs are added to the existing steam turbine. Was labeled as "partial conversion" in previous GOGPT data releases
not found

entries.name: Conversion from/replacement of (fuel)
entries.definition: Fuel that unit is converted from, or being replaced

entries.name: Conversion from/replacement of (GEM unit ID)
entries.definition: GEM Unit ID of unit that is being converted from, or being replaced

entries.name: Start year
entries.definition: Year for the unit's commissioning; For combined cycle sets, which are composed of multiple gas and steam turbines which may not have all been built at the same time, the start year reflects when they began combined cycle operation together.

entries.name: Retired year
entries.definition: Year the plant is decommissioned

entries.name: Planned retire
entries.definition: Future date for the unit's planned retirement

entries.name: Operator(s)
entries.definition: The company that is responsible for day-to-day operations at a plant. This is usually only one company.

entries.name: Owner(s)
entries.definition: The company that directly owns the project (as in, the lowest-level identified owner in the chain of ownership)

entries.name: Parent(s)
entries.definition: Highest-level project owner in the chain of ownership and ultimate parents (e.g., corporations, investment firms, and governments).

entries.name: Latitude
entries.definition: Latitude, assumed WGS84 (google maps)

entries.name: Longitude
entries.definition: Longitude, assumed WGS84 (google maps)

entries.name: Location accuracy
entries.definition: Location is "approximate" based by using nearest city or other information in research, or Location is "exact" based on visibility on map or given in research

entries.name: City
entries.definition: City, town, or village where project is located

entries.name: Local area (taluk, county)
entries.definition: county/taluk where project is located

entries.name: Major area (prefecture, district)
entries.definition: Prefecture or district where project is located

entries.name: State/Province
entries.definition: Province or state where project is located

entries.name: Subregion
entries.definition: Based on definitons of sub-regions from the United National Statistics Division M49: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/methodology/m49/

entries.name: Region
entries.definition: Based on definitons of sub-regions from the United National Statistics Division M49: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/methodology/m49/

entries.name: Other IDs (location)
entries.definition: For example, government database ID

entries.name: Other IDs (unit)
entries.definition: For example, government database ID

entries.name: Captive industry use
entries.definition: "Captive" power plants are those that are within industrial facilities. These power plants typically supply most of their power (and if they're CHP, most of their heat as well) to the industrial facility. Options include: heat, power, both.

entries.name: Captive industry type
entries.definition: The type of captive industry use. Options include: agriculture, aluminum, automobiles, cement & building, chemicals, coal mining & coal products, cryptocurrency, dairy, data center, electronics, hydrogen production, industrial park, iron & steel, machinery, nickel, nutrition, oil & refining, other, other metals & mining, pulp & paper, rubber, sugar, textiles, unknown

entries.name: Captive non-industry use
entries.definition: Indicate if heat or power is used outside of the captive industrial facility or complex. Options include: heat, power, both, none. 'None' indicates an entirely captive plant.

entries.name: Fuel classification?
entries.definition: Fuel classification options include: Oil only, LNG only, Gas only, Multi fuel.   Note "Gas only" does not include LNG.
